question:
[Previous Feedback] Great work! The blocker data has been successfully extracted into a structured format with all time fields properly formatted. This makes it much easier to track and analyze blocking issues.

In project management JSON files, which of the following time field values have the correct format?

A. "target_date": "2026-03-19T18:00:00+08:00"
B. "last_updated": "2026-03-17"
C. "discovered_at": "2026-03-17T09:15:00+08:00"
D. "deadline": "EOD Friday"
E. "expected_resolved_at": "2026-03-18T12:00:00+08:00"
F. "sprint_start": "2026-W12-1"

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...} format.

content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 11:09 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Great work! The blocker data has been successfully extracted into a structured format with all time fields properly formatted. This makes it much easier to track and analyze blocking issues.

In project management JSON files, which of the following time field values have the correct format?

A. "target_date": "2026-03-19T18:00:00+08:00"
B. "last_updated": "2026-03-17"
C. "discovered_at": "2026-03-17T09:15:00+08:00"
D. "deadline": "EOD Friday"
E. "expected_resolved_at": "2026-03-18T12:00:00+08:00"
F. "sprint_start": "2026-W12-1"

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...} format.

Based on the standard datetime format requirements (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS+08:00), the correctly formatted time fields are:

\bbox{A,C,E}

answer:
Based on the standard datetime format requirements (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS+08:00), the correctly formatted time fields are:

\bbox{A,C,E}
